$schema_note: Law #8: entries are append-only. passes flips true ONLY after the verification steps were executed against the running app with evidence. Removing or weakening entries is forbidden.

features.title: Money is integer cents at the schema level
verification: Apply all migrations to a blank Postgres, then run the information_schema scan: zero real/double precision columns exist anywhere and every *_cents column is bigint
Attempt INSERT of a fractional value into expenses.amount_cents via psql — rejected by type
CI scan test (money-law scan from DB-1 suite) is green in the pipeline run

features.title: Photos default to private visibility at the DB level
verification: Against the migrated DB, INSERT a photos row omitting visibility — persisted row reads visibility='private'
INSERT with explicit NULL visibility is rejected (NOT NULL)

features.title: Uniqueness invariants reject duplicates
verification: Against the migrated DB: second trip_members row with role='owner' for the same trip is rejected by the partial unique index

Duplicate places (source, source_id) rejected; duplicate travel leg (from,to,mode) rejected; second booking referencing the same capture_id rejected; duplicate (trip_id, place_id) saved place and tour bundle rejected

features.title: Check constraints reject malformed rows
verification: Against the migrated DB, each of these INSERTs fails on its CHECK: negative cents, lowercase currency, price_cents without currency, custom place with source_id set, itinerary item kind/column mismatch, from=to travel leg, ready bundle without content, self-settlement
expense_shares has no currency column (inherits parent expense currency)

features.title: Delete-behavior matrix holds (cascade / SET NULL / RESTRICT)
verification: Delete a fixture trip: all trip-scoped children cascade; capture_inbox.trip_id and documents.trip_id go NULL
Delete a booking: its itinerary item cascades, its linked expense survives with booking_id NULL
Attempt hard DELETE of a users row that has expenses/settlements — blocked by RESTRICT

features.title: User creation writes users + entitlements atomically
verification: Run the user-creation helper against the test DB: users row and entitlements row (plan='free') exist after commit
Inject a failure between the two writes: neither row persists (single transaction)

features.title: Enums and money scalars are single-source and strict
verification: Enum-parity snapshot test: every @gogo/shared tuple matches schema spec §3.2 value lists and the Drizzle pgEnums are built from the same tuples
Cents schema rejects 25.5 and -1; PositiveCents rejects 0; CurrencyCode rejects 'usd'
ISODate/ISODateTime/ISOTime reject epoch numbers and malformed strings

features.title: JSONB payloads are Zod-gatekept (booking details, expense create)
verification: BookingDetails discriminated union: a valid payload per each of the 8 categories parses; details/category mismatch rejected; unknown keys stripped
ExpenseCreate superRefine: shares summing != amount rejected, exact sum accepted
UserPrefs parse strips unknown keys

features.title: Shared privacy, AI and entitlement helpers behave
verification: canViewPhoto truth table passes: owner/member/stranger x private/trip/public (Law #3 single implementation)
AI-schema constraint walker: every ai/* schema has no recursion, no numeric range constraints, depth <= 3
deriveAiCacheKey is stable and changes when any input incl. SCHEMA_VERSION changes; travel_style canonicalization (sorted, empty -> 'any') proven
resolveEntitlements: override beats plan default; dependency-cruiser/lint check proves @gogo/shared imports nothing platform-bound

features.title: Appearance: OS scheme, manual pref, no dark-launch flash
verification: Simulator with OS dark mode + no stored pref: app boots dark with no light flash on first frame (record launch video and inspect)
Set manual pref 'light', relaunch: app renders light despite OS dark; pref survives relaunch
Pref 'system': flip OS appearance while app is open — all mounted screens re-render in the new scheme without restart

features.title: Accent themes: 3 registered, runtime-swappable, data-only
verification: Theme picker lists default + 2 alternates; selecting one re-renders mounted screens immediately and persists across relaunch
Add a throwaway AccentThemeDef to the registry in a scratch branch: it appears and renders with zero component/screen code changes

features.title: WCAG AA contrast matrix passes for every scheme x accent
verification: Run the tokens contrast-matrix test: every declared fg/bg pair >= 4.5:1 body / 3:1 large-UI across light+dark x all 3 accents — green in CI output pasted as evidence

features.title: Token-only styling is enforced (no literal colors in screens)
verification: Introduce a literal hex color and a bare StyleSheet.create in a screen file on a scratch branch: pnpm lint fails on both
createStyles returns referentially stable styles per Theme (memo test)

features.title: Core components behave per contract
verification: In the running Gallery: Button shows pressed feedback <100ms and loading blocks re-press with stable width
ConfirmDialog executes only on explicit confirm; cancel is a no-op
Sheet supports swipe-down AND explicit close; screen-reader focus moves in on open and restores on close
Skeleton renders on initial fetch, EmptyState on zero items, ErrorBanner with working retry on failure (drive with a stubbed failing query)

features.title: Accessibility floor: hit targets, Dynamic Type, reduce motion
verification: Snapshot UI in simulator: every interactive element hit target >= 44x44pt (small icons via hitSlop)
Crank OS text size to max: typography scales per role caps without clipping single-line chrome
Enable reduce-motion: skeleton shimmer becomes static, entrance slides become fades
Interactive components expose accessibilityRole/Label; omitting testID on an interactive component is a type error (compile check)

features.title: Gallery screen renders every component x variant x scheme x accent
verification: Open the dev Gallery in the simulator and walk all components across both schemes and all 3 accents — screenshots captured as evidence (Law #7 visual verification surface)

features.title: Route skeleton: full tree, per-tab stacks, modal conventions, testID grammar
verification: Every route in the nav spec §2.1 tree mounts a placeholder screen in the simulator without redbox
Push into a detail on one tab, switch tabs and return: stack and scroll position preserved per tab
Forms present modally, detail drill-downs push (spot-check both conventions)
testID lint (NAV-7) fails on a scratch interactive element missing testID; screen roots expose <screen>-screen

features.title: Apple sign-in end-to-end (verify, create, return)
verification: Drive POST /auth/apple with a new-user token fixture: users + entitlements rows created in one txn, is_new_user=true, ES256 access token carries sub/sid, refresh stored hashed
Repeat with the same apple_sub: same user id, is_new_user=false, new session row
Tampered signature, wrong aud, expired token, nonce mismatch all return 401 with indistinguishable bodies
Apple code-exchange failure still signs in (logged, not fatal); on-device Apple sign-in completes into the app

features.title: Google sign-in + auto-link on verified email
verification: POST /auth/google with new + returning fixtures behaves as Apple equivalents keyed on google_sub
Unknown google_sub whose verified email matches an Apple-created account: google_sub set on the existing row, signed in, no second account
email_verified=false collision returns 401 with no link and no new account

features.title: Refresh rotation with reuse-theft family revocation
verification: POST /auth/refresh with a valid token: old token stamped rotated_at, new pair returned, session last_used_at bumped — atomically
Present the already-rotated token: 401 AND the whole session's tokens revoked; the newer 'legitimate' token now also 401s
Expired-but-never-rotated token: plain 401, session NOT revoked
DB inspection: only SHA-256 hashes stored, no plaintext refresh tokens anywhere

features.title: Stateless requireAuth, fixed middleware order, rate limits, uniform errors
verification: Protected route with alg=none / HS256 / bad iss / bad aud / expired token: all 401; verification does no DB read (assert via query counter)
Invalid token + invalid body returns 401 (auth precedes validation)
Each auth-surface rate limit returns 429 + Retry-After at threshold and resets after the window
Every failure serializes as the ApiError envelope with an ErrorCode enum member; validation failures carry zod flatten() details; success bodies are the documented schema with no wrapper

features.title: Sessions: list, revoke, logout
verification: Sign in on two simulators: GET /auth/sessions lists both with current=true exactly once; the settings screen renders them
Revoke the other device's session: its refresh 401s immediately; access works only until exp (<=15 min documented latency)
POST /auth/logout revokes the calling session and deletes the supplied push token; another user's session id returns 404

features.title: Client auth gate: splash hold, redirect + stash/resume, sign-out reset, secure storage
verification: Cold launch signed-out: no authed-screen flash before sign-in renders (splash holds through hydration)
Navigate to a protected route while signed out: redirected to sign-in, then landed on the intended destination after auth
Sign out: navigation state reset, stashes cleared, lands on sign-in; query cache empty
Refresh token lives in expo-secure-store only (not AsyncStorage/MMKV/TQ persist — code + device inspection); access token memory-only

features.title: First-run onboarding flow
verification: Fresh account sign-in routes to onboarding: name/avatar -> home currency -> payment handles (skippable) -> notification priming -> optional travel_style
Every collected field round-trips through GET /users/me after completion
Skipping every skippable step still lands on the trip list; skipped fields editable later in profile

features.title: Profile edit + avatar presigned upload
verification: GET /users/me returns the full User shape; PATCH display_name + prefs round-trips with unknown pref keys stripped
Avatar flow in the app: presign ticket (key namespaced avatars/{user_id}/, TTL <= 10 min) -> PUT bytes -> commit key -> avatar renders
Committing a key issued to another user (or never issued) returns 400; email/provider subs/forward_email_slug are not client-writable

features.title: Payment handles: normalization, validation, no Venmo traffic
verification: Save '@sean' and '$seantokuzo': stored and returned bare; charset violations 400
cashtag HEAD 404 -> 400 with details.cashtag='not_found'; cash.app 5xx/timeout -> save succeeds (fail-open)
zelle_handle without zelle_display_name -> 400; non-email/non-E.164 zelle -> 400
Mocked transport asserts zero outbound requests to any venmo.com host during validation

features.title: Push token registration, keep-alive, move semantics
verification: Register a token: row created; re-register same token: same id, last_seen_at bumped
Register a token previously owned by account B as account A: token moves (owned by A only, not duplicated)
DELETE of another user's push-token id returns 404 and touches nothing

features.title: Effective entitlements read via the shared resolver
verification: GET /users/me/entitlements on a fresh free account returns ai_calls_per_day=30 etc. from PLAN_DEFAULTS
Set overrides.ai_calls_per_day=100 in DB: endpoint returns 100 (resolver precedence)
No entitlement write endpoint exists (route inventory check)

features.title: Account deletion: fixed effects + soft-delete/PII scrub
verification: DELETE /users/me: all sessions + refresh tokens revoked, push tokens deleted, Apple revocation endpoint called (mocked assert), device signed out
DB shows the users row surviving with deleted_at set and PII scrubbed (email tombstone, 'Deleted user' name, handles/subs/slug NULL, prefs {})
Full sign-in/refresh/logout/delete cycle log sweep contains no token material

features.title: Create trip: owner membership txn, structured destination, required dates
verification: In the app: create-trip modal requires name, destination via structured search (Overture city subset; picking a result fills lat/lng), and a date range — no free-text destination path exists
Submit: trips row + creator owner membership in one txn (DB check); app lands inside the new trip on the itinerary tab
Failure path preserves entered values with ErrorBanner; dirty dismissal intercepts with a discard confirm
base_currency defaulted from home_currency ?? USD without occupying the form

features.title: Trip list: status sections, sorting, empty state, freshness
verification: Seed active/planning/past trips: list groups Happening now -> Upcoming -> Past with correct intra-section sort; rows show name/destination/dates/member count
GET /trips returns only membership trips, each with the caller's role
Zero-trip account renders EmptyState with create + join CTAs; foregrounding the app refetches stale queries (observe network)

features.title: Trip settings edit with LWW + expect_updated_at conflict UX
verification: Editor edits name/dates/destination/theme; viewer sees those rows hidden/disabled and the server 403s a forced attempt
Two-device edit race: second save with stale expect_updated_at gets 409, client refetches, re-renders fresh values with a 'review and re-save' notice — no silent overwrite
Mutations return the full updated row (client reconciles without a follow-up GET)

features.title: Trip status: date-derived with owner override (archive)
verification: Trip with today inside dates reads status active; move dates to the past + run reconciliation: status flips to past
Owner sets override to 'past' on a current trip: effective status past, reconciliation skips it; clearing the override recomputes from dates
Non-owner attempting the override gets 403; server and client agree on the boundary day via the shared derived_status helper

features.title: Members: role ladder, role change, transfer, removal/leave, member profiles
verification: Members screen: viewer sees no manage affordances, editor sees invite only, owner sees all; forced 403 renders ErrorBanner not a crash
Role change editor<->viewer works; role endpoint can never grant/revoke owner; transfer demotes owner to editor and promotes target in one txn
Owner leave blocked with transfer-first guidance (409 from API); non-owner leave works; removal requires ConfirmDialog and evicted member loses access on next request
GET /users/:userId returns UserProfile (with payment handles) for a co-member and byte-identical 404 for stranger/nonexistent; DELETE /users/me while owning a shared trip returns 409

features.title: Trip deletion cascades and force-exits other members
verification: Owner deletes trip through the destructive ConfirmDialog: API cascades children per the schema matrix (DB check)
A second signed-in member inside that trip receives trip.deleted invalidation and is exited to the trip list with a notice
Editor/viewer delete attempts 403

features.title: Invites: multi-use links, expiry, revoke, max_uses race, idempotent accept
verification: Create invite (role <= creator's, never owner; >=128-bit token) and share: second account joins via the link; use_count increments; preview renders before any membership change
Concurrent accepts against max_uses=1 never exceed the cap (race test); existing-member accept is a no-op returning current membership
Expired/revoked/maxed tokens return CONFLICT with distinct details.reason and the client renders distinct error states; unknown token 404s with no oracle
Owner revokes any invite, editor only their own

features.title: Invite deep links: cold/warm parity, stash-resume, malformed fallback
verification: Open an invite link with the app killed and again warm: both land on the join screen identically
Open while signed out: auth flow runs, then the join screen resumes with authz re-checked
Accept lands inside the trip with default-tab rules; malformed/unknown links land on the trip list with a non-blocking notice, never a crash

features.title: IDOR posture: non-members get indistinguishable 404s everywhere
verification: Non-member calls trip-scoped endpoints with a real tripId and a random uuid: byte-identical 404 bodies (reusable authz harness output pasted)
Deep link to a non-member trip renders the no-access state with zero trip data fetched into the UI (network log)
A member below the required role gets 403 (membership already proves existence)

features.title: Entry redirect, default tabs, in-session tab memory, multi-active landing
verification: No active trip: cold launch lands on the trip list; exactly one active trip: lands on its today tab
Opening an active trip defaults to today; planning/past trip defaults to itinerary; manual tab choice sticks for the session and resets on relaunch
Two active trips: cold launch lands on the most-recently-viewed one's today tab (trip list if none viewed); header trip switcher moves between active trips

features.title: POI spine ingestion: on-demand, idempotent, deduped, failure-visible
verification: Create a trip for a fresh destination: region cells enqueue and ingest Overture + FSQ OS rows; place_ingest_regions rows track status
Re-trigger the same region: upsert is idempotent (no duplicates, no deletes); FSQ record within dedup distance of an Overture record loses
Force a source failure: region row shows failed + error (ops-visible), trip creation unaffected
Region younger than 90 days skips re-ingest; older refreshes

features.title: Place search (spine-first) + custom places
verification: GET /places/search returns spine hits for text + geo-biased queries (trgm-backed); coverage miss returns gracefully and enqueues backfill ingest
Custom places appear only for members of trips where they are referenced — stranger searches never see them
Create a custom place (name+coords): only its creator can PATCH/DELETE it (others 403/404 per posture)

features.title: Collab loop: ids-only push events, optimistic writes with rollback, invalidation map
verification: Every §3.5 domain event fires post-commit to members except the actor; payload carries event/trip_id/entity_id only — no content, no PII (captured payload evidence)
Role change applied optimistically rolls back visibly on a forced 500
member.removed targeting me evicts the trip subtree and exits with a notice; invite.created/revoked invalidates the invite list

features.title: Booking lifecycle: typed per-category details, status machine, cancel keeps history
verification: Create one booking per category through the app: details validated against the shared union (invalid shape 400), price requires currency
Category change attempt on update is rejected; starts_at/ends_at denormalize from details times

Walk the status machine idea->planned->booked and ->cancelled: illegal transitions rejected; cancelled booking drops off the calendar but keeps its row and expense links (visible under show-cancelled)

features.title: Booking <-> calendar item single source of truth
verification: Create a timed booking: its itinerary item appears automatically on the right day; edit the booking's times: the item moves in the same transaction
Schedule a timeless booking via the schedule endpoint / Add-to-day flow: item created, status advances idea->planned
Delete the booking-kind item: booking survives, returns to the unscheduled bucket

features.title: Ideas bucket: off-calendar candidates with scheduling flow
verification: Idea bookings never render in day sections; the pinned Ideas entry shows a correct count badge and groups cards by category
'Add to day' picker schedules the card into its day optimistically with the badge advancing to planned
Timeless planned/booked bookings show the 'needs a day' flag; cancelled bookings hide behind the Show-cancelled toggle

features.title: Plan-mode day list: sections, empty-day rows, view-toggle persistence
verification: Day list covers the trip range with items ordered by sort_order; empty days render the slim 'Add to this day' row
Booking items show category icon + status badge (planned=accent, booked=success)
Toggle list<->grid: choice persists per trip across app relaunch; initial-load skeletons and the zero-content EmptyState render

features.title: Drag reorder with booking-day locks and rollback
verification: Long-press drag within and across days persists via the day-order PUT and survives refetch; haptics fire on lift/drop
Forced server failure rolls the order back visibly with an ErrorBanner
A timed booking's item refuses cross-day drop with the inline hint; same-day reorder still works

features.title: Travel times end-to-end: leg computation, chips, degradation, refresh
verification: Add two located consecutive items: legs compute async (mutation latency unaffected — measure) and the travel-time chip appears with the R-itin-5 default mode
Chip tap lists every computed mode + a Directions handoff opening Apple/Google Maps; absent transit shows no error (rows simply missing)
Reorder the day: stale legs for broken adjacencies are cleaned, new pairs computed (DB matches the adjacency contract)
Provider outage: itinerary mutations still succeed; chips degrade silently; refresh endpoint rate-limits

features.title: Overlaps surfaced, never blocked: list chips + grid side-by-side + sort-by-time
verification: Create two overlapping timed items: both save (legal), list shows warning chips on both, grid renders them side-by-side with overlap badges — never occluded
Non-monotonic day ordering offers one-tap 'Sort day by time' which issues the day-order PUT; nothing auto-resorts

features.title: Calendar grid: hour axis, positioned blocks, gap-tap add, all-day lane
verification: Grid mode pages day columns against a shared 00-24 hour axis; timed items are positioned/sized by start/end; block tap opens detail
Tapping an empty slot opens the add flow prefilled with that day + time rounded to 30 min
Untimed items render as chips in the all-day lane; grid opens on day 1 (or today when active) with the 08:00-20:00 band visible

features.title: Multi-day bookings render as one spanning item
verification: Multi-night lodging booking: grid shows one spanning all-day lane across covered columns; day list synthesizes check-in and check-out point rows only (nights between show nothing) — both route to the same booking detail
DB holds exactly one itinerary row (day + end_day), never two
Cross-midnight flight renders clipped at midnight with the +1 tail

features.title: Add/edit flows for all 10 types with inline conflict notice
verification: FAB opens the add sheet with 8 categories + place visit + custom; each type's form mirrors its details shape
Saving without times lands in the Ideas bucket; saving with times auto-schedules; overlapping choice shows a non-blocking inline notice and still saves
Place-visit picker offers saved places first then spine search; direct item creation only allows place_visit/custom kinds (API check)

features.title: Deeplink-out loop: exact partner URLs, recording, return prompt
verification: Each partner button builds its exact §2.7 URL (snapshot per partner: Kayak/Skyscanner date formats, Airbnb/Booking/Expedia/Vrbo params, Trainline URN two-step, Turo MM/DD/YYYY); buttons disable with a missing-field hint until required fields exist
adults defaults to trip member count and an inline edit flows into the constructed URL
Tapping records {partner, category, tripId, timestamp} then opens externally; foregrounding within 30 min shows 'Did you book it?' exactly once
Prompt's add-manually reopens the add flow prefilled and the created booking carries source='deeplink_return'

features.title: Booking & item detail screens + composite read + viewer read-only
verification: Booking detail renders per-category field grid, confirmation-code copy (mono), price, source label, linked place/expenses/schedule rows; cancel/delete run their ConfirmDialogs with correct copy
place_visit/custom items open the item detail; booking-kind items route to booking detail (no duplicated surface)
The itinerary renders from the one-shot composite read (items + legs, single request per range — network log)
Viewer role: all itinerary/booking mutations 403 server-side; edit affordances absent in UI

features.title: Map pin layers, clustering, day filter
verification: Trip with saved places + scheduled items: map renders saved pins and day-color-coded itinerary pins from cached trip data
Zoom out to force clustering: clusters show counts; cluster tap expands, never opens a sheet
Day filter chip shows only that day's itinerary pins (others dimmed) and recenters the camera; chips match mapDayColors and pins carry day numbers

features.title: Map theming and attribution compliance
verification: Switch light/dark: matching custom style loads and pin/route colors derive from Theme (no literal colors — lint + visual check)
Mapbox wordmark + attribution visible and unobscured by pills/FABs/sheet at rest; attribution info sheet lists spine attribution strings

features.title: Pin interactions: place sheet, external nav handoff, map<->itinerary linking
verification: Pin tap opens the place sheet (name, category, save toggle, actions); map tap dismisses
Navigate hands off to Apple/Google Maps with the coordinates — no in-app turn-by-turn
Add-to-day opens the itinerary add modal prefilled kind=place_visit + place_id
View-in-itinerary cross-tab pushes to the item detail with tab stacks intact; itinerary->map focuses the pin, opens its sheet once, and the param is not re-consumed on tab revisit

features.title: Place detail: spine view, note editing, linked content, silent fresh degrade
verification: Detail screen renders spine data, editable saved-place note (owner/editor), linked itinerary items and this-trip photos with cross-navigation, and source attribution footer
Fresh premium block absent (offline/error/deferred-MVP): full spine view renders with no error surface
With a stubbed fresh payload: TQ persister snapshot contains no place-fresh entry after use; nothing written to SQLite/MMKV/logs (grep + snapshot evidence)

features.title: Map search bar with temporary result pins
verification: >=2 chars queries GET /places/search debounced and geo-biased; results render as temporary pins + list
Result tap opens the standard place sheet (save/add-to-day work); clearing removes temporary pins
Offline: search entry degrades with an offline notice, no unresolvable spinner

features.title: Foreground-only location: lazy permission, no background keys
verification: No permission prompt on map mount; first locate-me tap prompts when-in-use once
Denied: map fully functional without puck, locate-me offers a Settings path with no re-prompt loop; granted: camera flies to the user with pins intact
Built app's Info.plist contains only NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription — no always keys, no background location modes (inspect the built plist)

features.title: Offline tile packs: lifecycle, hygiene, airplane-mode map
verification: Trip flips to active on wifi: style + tile pack download starts exactly once with progress in the status pill; on cellular it defers and resumes on the next wifi+active window
Trip-settings management UI shows none/downloading/ready/stale/failed with download (cellular size-confirm), refresh, delete; failure marks failed with working retry and the map stays usable online
Trip delete/leave removes its packs; past-trip purge frees count before a new download near the 750 ceiling
Airplane mode inside the downloaded region: tiles + pins + place sheet all work; search/fresh entry points show offline notices

features.title: Saved places: role-gated CRUD with save-once semantics
verification: Editor saves/unsaves a place and edits its note; viewer sees state but has no save control and the server 403s a forced write
Saving an already-saved place returns 409 which the client treats as success (saved state, no error UI)
Non-member saved-place requests 404 indistinguishable

features.title: Add expense: integer cents, atomic shares, member-only participants
verification: Amount input parses '25.50'->2550 and JPY '2550'->2550 via the shared minor-unit helper; floats fail wire validation (send 25.5 -> 400)
Create expense with shares: expenses + expense_shares written in one transaction; shares not summing to amount rejected server-side regardless of client math
Participant outside the trip 400s; payer may be any member; a viewer-role member can log an expense (and edit/delete their own) but cannot touch budgets
Kill the server mid-write (txn test): neither expense nor shares persist

features.title: Split engine: four types, deterministic remainders, client-server parity
verification: Equal/exact/percent/shares editors each show live per-member previews from the shared computeShares; save blocked until the split resolves exactly (remaining readout at zero, percents sum 100)
Property test: client preview === shared computeShares output across random inputs; remainder cents assigned by largest-remainder deterministically (same input -> same shares)
$100.00 across 3 members: shares are 3334/3333/3333 (or per pinned algorithm) and sum exactly

features.title: Multi-currency FX capture + base-currency lock
verification: Log a non-base-currency expense online: rate auto-fetched, base amount prefilled, manual override respected; stored fx_rate/base_amount_cents never re-fetched after write
Offline or FX-source failure: save requires manual rate entry

Trip with zero expenses: owner changes base currency and existing budget rows' currency updates in the same txn; after the first expense exists the change returns 409 and the settings row renders locked with an explainer

features.title: Expense list, detail, edit + the invalidation trio
verification: Expenses segment lists newest-first with payer, logged currency and 'your share'; member/category filters work; infinite scroll follows the cursor
Booking-link picker prefills amount/description/category per the fixed mapping; edit mode reopens with exact shares and PATCHes full replacement
Any expense/settlement mutation refreshes expenses AND balances AND budgets together (observe all three queries invalidate)
Empty states render (no expenses / all settled / untouched budget)

features.title: Expense soft-delete with visible audit trail
verification: Creator deletes own expense via ConfirmDialog: row gets deleted_at/deleted_by (never removed), balances and budget spend exclude it
History shows the audit entry ('X deleted ...'); owner can delete any expense, a non-creator editor cannot (403)
No hard-delete path exists for expenses (API surface check)

features.title: Balances: computed pairwise nets in base currency, ex-members included
verification: Fixture trip (multi-payer, multi-currency, settlements): balances endpoint returns pairwise nets matching hand-computed base-currency totals; nothing stored (no balance table)
FX expenses allocate via base_amount_cents
Remove a member with a nonzero balance: their expenses/shares survive and balances still render them for remaining members
Balances segment shows headline position, member chips, actionable transfer rows

features.title: Simplify debts: off by default, one-tap view toggle
verification: Transfer list renders pairwise by default; the toggle switches to the API's simplified array (fewer transfers, same net positions — verify totals) and back
Toggle state is per-view, not persisted across mounts

features.title: Settlements: record-only ledger, party rule, correction window
verification: Either party records a settlement; a third member attempting for others is rejected; rows carry no external transaction ids or payment state (schema + payload check)
Settlement commits in trip base currency and balances reflect it immediately
Recorder deletes own settlement within 24h; after 24h deletion is rejected (counter-entry is the correction path)

features.title: Settle-up rails: exact URL formats and gating
verification: Settle screen headlines the pairwise position with editable amount (over-amount warns, partial allowed); one button per handle the counterparty actually has — no disabled stubs, hint when zero handles

Link builder snapshots match §2.5 verbatim: venmo:// pay URL (web fallback when canOpenURL fails), cash.app/$tag/amount, paypal.me amount with pinned currency, JPY zero-decimal formatting; Zelle renders copyable handle + display name
Non-USD base trip hides Venmo/CashApp/Zelle, keeps PayPal + mark-as-settled; PayPal copy frames Friends & Family
Rail open failure shows a non-blocking error and the screen stays usable; handles are sourced only from trip-member UserProfile

features.title: Mark-as-settled always works + payment return prompt
verification: Mark-as-settled renders and records (method/amount/note sheet) with zero handles and zero rails
Rail deeplink-out then foreground within 30 min: 'Did you complete the payment?' sheet appears exactly once, prefilled with rail + amount; confirm posts the settlement, decline clears the stash
Creditor view flips the headline and offers Request payment + mark-as-settled (money received)

features.title: Send-the-bill: request create, share, recipient screen, resolution
verification: Creditor creates a request: share sheet opens with the universal link + message text; open requests annotate the balances rows with cancel via ConfirmDialog

Recipient (member) opens the link cold and warm: request screen renders requester, amount, the requester's rails, mark-as-settled; paying through it links the settlement and flips the request to settled for both parties
Settled/cancelled/unknown request ids render resolved/empty states with no pay buttons; a non-member opener gets the no-access state
Request detail discloses only the §minimum fields (payload inspection)

features.title: Budgets: per-category caps + overall cap vs actual spend
verification: Budget segment shows one row per expense_category (list derived from the shared enum) with cap, AI-estimate column, actual spend and progress; header shows editable overall trip cap (editor+) with computed total
Warning styling at >=80% of cap, over-budget styling past 100% (drive with fixture expenses)
Caps persist via the budgets upsert; viewer cannot edit caps (403 + hidden control)

features.title: AI platform gates: key custody, caps, ceilings, order, typed degradation
verification: Mobile workspace dependency scan shows no Anthropic SDK/key; CI env asserts no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; all model calls originate server-side
Drive an AI endpoint to the cap: 29th counted call passes, 30th returns 429 AI_CAP_EXCEEDED; per-feature ceilings trip independently; gate runs before any model call (mock assert)
Structured outputs parse against the shared schema with the server-side refine step; invalid output after retry returns 503 AI_UPSTREAM
Client maps AI_CAP_EXCEEDED / AI_DISABLED / AI_UPSTREAM to friendly non-blocking states and AI content carries the disclosure treatment

features.title: Usage accounting, integer cost math, $50 alert / $100 kill-switch
verification: Each model call upserts ai_usage (calls + token counts) on (user, feature, day); cache hits write nothing
Cost fixtures: tokens x price table compute in integer microcents end-to-end (no float types in the pipeline); batch features half-priced
Spend fixture >= $100: every AI endpoint returns 503 AI_DISABLED and batch submission refuses; month rollover reopens; >= $50 fires the ops alert once

features.title: Destination cache: shared, anonymous, cache-first
verification: Two different users with the same destination/style/season: second request serves from ai_cache with no model call, no usage row, no cap decrement (assert via mock + counters)
ai_cache rows contain no user_id/trip_id (schema + row inspection); expired rows evicted by the sweep
SCHEMA_VERSION bump changes the key and busts the stale shape

features.title: Anti-hallucination contract: grounding, cite-or-retract, no volatile facts
verification: Snapshot every generative template: grounding block with provided facts/ids, restriction clause, unknown-permission clause, no banned brevity phrases
Refiner drops output items whose place_id is outside the candidate set and facts whose source_ref does not resolve (fixture proof)
Schema walker finds zero volatile-fact fields (hours/prices) across ai/* outputs; pre-gen verification pass runs for batch content

features.title: Recommendations grounded in the spine
verification: Request recommendations for a seeded destination: every returned item references a real spine place_id from the candidate list; capped at 20
Cards render with save-to-trip working (saved place appears on the map); repeat request hits the destination cache
Destination with too few candidate places degrades with insufficient_places, not invented venues

features.title: AI expense estimation writes budgets, client CTA state machine
verification: Editor taps Estimate with AI: gate order (kill-switch -> entitlement -> cache) holds, per-category integer ranges validate server-side, budgets rows persist ai_estimate_cents + ai_estimated_at and the UI refetches to show them
Cache key includes canonicalized travel_style; second user same destination/style hits cache
CTA states: disabled offline, loading blocks re-press, AI_CAP_EXCEEDED and AI_DISABLED map to friendly copy; viewer sees no CTA (403 server-side)

features.title: Tour guide: T-3 batch pre-gen, reconciliation, offline bundles, surfacing
verification: Trip entering T-3 days (or manual Prepare offline): batch submits one bundle per saved place idempotently within the structural cap; reconciliation flips pending->ready/failed from batch results in any order
Ready bundles download into device SQLite over wifi; airplane-mode lookup by place_id renders full bundle content with per-fact source refs
Place detail shows the tour-guide entry only when a ready bundle exists; radius surfacing fires once per place per day and only while the tour surface is foregrounded
Failed batch items are visible as failed rows, never silent; kill-switch blocks submission

features.title: Packing-list generation (live, uncached)
verification: POST /ai/packing-list for a trip: inputs are destination/dates/weather/travel_style; returns PackingItem[] without checked, nothing persisted by the endpoint (DB unchanged until the client saves)
Counts against the packing feature ceiling (5/day) — 6th call 429s
No cache row written (live/uncached by design)

features.title: Post-trip recap: one per trip, grounded, privacy-filtered highlights
verification: Trip transitions to past: recap batch submits once (re-trigger is idempotent — unique per trip); reconciliation lands content in recaps
Prompt snapshot contains only server-computed facts (stats/trace/highlight ids) — no photo content or private-photo metadata with a mixed-visibility fixture
Highlight selection is deterministic (same inputs -> same ids) and rendered highlights pass canViewPhoto for the viewer

features.title: Forward address provisioning + capture onboarding
verification: First onboarding open provisions the address (idempotent slug) with a loading state; failure shows ErrorBanner with working retry — never a fake address
Address renders with one-tap copy + toast; teaching covers forward path (Apple Mail 3-tap, Gmail-must-forward) and share path
The 'Did you book it?' prompt's how-to-forward action opens this same screen; it stays reachable from the queue header afterward

features.title: Email ingest: webhook auth, slug routing, sender policy, parse-reply
verification: Unsigned/misauthenticated webhook posts are rejected; a real forwarded email to <slug>@in.<domain> lands a pending capture_inbox row with raw MIME in object storage
From an unverified sender: bounced per policy; add + verify an additional sender (token link) and the same forward now lands
Duplicate Message-ID delivery does not create a second row; oversized email rejected at the cap
Parse-reply email fires once per email capture with the correct filed/needs-review/failed template (no reply for share captures)

features.title: Share-sheet ingestion: cold/warm routing, limits, never dropped
verification: Share a PDF, an image, text, and a URL from other apps on cold AND warm start: each routes through auth gating into the queue with a processing row
Share while signed out: payload stashed, auth runs, upload resumes
413/400/429/network failures render the synthetic row with reason + retry; discard requires ConfirmDialog; the payload is never silently dropped (kill the app mid-failure and relaunch — still there)

features.title: Parse pipeline: JSON-LD first, LLM fallback, confidence routing, trip inference
verification: JSON-LD fixtures per reservation type parse to the right category/details with parser='jsonld'; a no-JSON-LD fixture invokes the Haiku fallback
High/medium/low LLM confidence routes to parsed vs needs_review at the pinned threshold; the heuristic gate (no dates + no confirmation code) forces needs_review even at high confidence
Trip inference guesses only at exactly one date-overlapping trip (viewer-role trips excluded)
Kill-switch/capture-ceiling on: JSON-LD still parses, LLM misses land needs_review with ai_unavailable; every failure stage writes a visible failed/needs_review row

features.title: Auto-file with push and one-tap undo
verification: High-confidence capture with a single trip guess auto-files: booking + capture link created in one transaction and the push notification carries the undo action
Undo (push action or landed-row overflow): booking removed, capture returns to needs_review for normal review; 409 (booking edited/deleted since) shows the explanatory toast + refresh
Auto-filed rows render in landed history as 'Filed to <trip>' linking to the booking; no auto-file happens without a guess

features.title: Review queue: states, polling, badge
verification: Queue lists captures newest-first with all five row treatments (pending/parsed/needs_review/failed/landed); segment switches open vs landed history
A pending row updates in place within the 3s poll window; polling stops after 90s falling back to refetch-on-focus
Trip-list header badge equals needs_review + failed + unlanded parsed; the per-trip More entry shows the same queue filtered to that trip; empty queue renders the onboarding empty state

features.title: Review flows: confirm lands atomically, reject, reparse, raw view
verification: Review screen renders the proposal (category/title/times/price/confirmation + parser provenance) with trip picker (owner/editor trips only, date-overlapping first, guess preselected)
Edited fields ride confirm as overrides; 201 creates the booking atomically with capture_id linked and navigates to it; 400 shows inline field errors; 409 flips the row to landed
Reject warns the original is deleted, then removes capture + raw object; failed captures show error + retry/add-manually/reject and reparse returns the row to processing
View-original opens the signed raw URL; an expired URL is transparently refetched once; another user's capture ids 404

features.title: Capture privacy: PII-free logs, SSRF guard, 30-day raw retention
verification: Run a full email + share capture cycle and sweep logs: no subjects, bodies, addresses, or parsed PII beyond ids/statuses
Shared URLs targeting private/link-local/metadata addresses are refused (unit + integration proof, redirects re-checked)
Confirming a capture deletes the raw object and NULLs raw_ref; the daily sweep purges raw payloads older than 30 days while queue rows + proposals survive
Capture rows never appear in any other member's surface

features.title: Two-phase photo upload with limits
verification: Album FAB offers camera + multi-select library; chosen photos enqueue and return control immediately (no network block)
Pipeline mints slots (batch cap enforced), PUTs bytes, finalizes: photos row appears with blurhash + dimensions; finalize retry for the same slot is idempotent (one row)
Oversized/unaccepted files rejected at selection with per-item messages; server rejects >MAX_PHOTO_BYTES and disallowed MIME with 413/400

features.title: Offline-tolerant upload queue with visible failures
verification: Enqueue uploads in airplane mode, kill the app, relaunch, restore connectivity: uploads complete (queue persisted, bytes referenced not copied)
Pending/failed tiles render in the grid with progress, retry, cancel; a deleted local asset marks its item failed with an explanation, never crashes
Expired ticket/PUT URL transparently re-mints on retry

features.title: Location consent: per-upload opt-in, priming before every OS prompt
verification: Camera, library, and location primes each render before their OS dialog on first use; denial of one path leaves the other functional with a settings explainer
Consent off: extract_location=false on the wire (proxy the finalize call) and no GPS is read client-side; server persists no lat/lng even when EXIF has GPS
After first consent the choice becomes the remembered default toggle, still flippable per upload; manual pinning works either way

features.title: Visibility boundary enforced on every photo read
verification: Fixture trip with private/trip/public photos from two members: member B's gallery, viewer deep link, and pin feed never contain member A's private photo (404-indistinguishable on direct fetch)
Coordinates are redacted from serialized rows wherever the viewer could not see location (payload inspection)
Public-by-place endpoint returns only public photos in the redacted PublicPlacePhoto shape (no uploader PII, no exact GPS) and renders on the place detail sheet for a non-member
Uploads without explicit visibility land private end-to-end

features.title: Visibility control UX: explicit widen, frictionless narrow, owner-only
verification: Owner sees pin/caption/visibility/delete controls; non-owners see read-only metadata with controls absent (not disabled); server 403s a non-owner visibility PATCH
Widening to public opens the ConfirmDialog stating scope + no-recall; cancel leaves visibility unchanged; confirm is not the default-focused action
Narrowing applies instantly (optimistic, rollback on error) and the previously-visible photo disappears from other members' surfaces on their next fetch

features.title: Trip album: day and place grouping (the trip journal)
verification: By-day grid groups by taken_at in trip-day terms matching itinerary day headers (created_at fallback covered); by-place segment groups under pinned places with an Unpinned section
Tiles show blurhash placeholders then thumbs; trip/public badges overlay (private unmarked); empty album renders EmptyState with upload CTA
Active-trip album renders offline from cached metadata + thumbs; full-size loads degrade with retry

features.title: Pin editing and suggestions that never auto-pin
verification: Finalize response suggestions surface as one-tap 'Pin to <place>?' chips; confirm PATCHes the pin, dismiss writes nothing (network log)
Viewer pin editor offers saved places + same-day itinerary items with suggestions preselected; caption edit round-trips
GPS-less photo yields no place suggestion; unpinned photos offer the suggest-pins action

features.title: Photo pins on the map are visibility-safe
verification: Map photo pins derive from the server-filtered gallery query restricted to located rows; a second member's map never receives coordinates for photos they cannot see (inspect the pin data source)
Photo-pin tap opens the place/day-filtered album; cluster of photo pins routes the same way

features.title: Photo deletion, owner moderation, departed members, storage GC
verification: Uploader deletes own photo via ConfirmDialog: optimistic removal, row + renditions gone, restored visibly on server error
Trip owner deletes another member's photo (moderation); an editor cannot
A departed member's photos remain in the trip and the departed uploader retains delete rights on return via public surfaces per spec
GC job: orphaned storage objects (PUT without finalize) reclaimed; missing-object rows flagged (run the job against fixtures)

features.title: Today surface: timeline, hero countdown, leave-by, day states, quick actions
verification: Active-trip day with timed items: chronological timeline with legs interleaved, done items collapsed/dimmed, the in-progress item marked Now, the next item as hero with a live countdown refreshing every minute
Hero with a preceding leg shows leave-by = start - leg - 10min buffer (matches the shared computation); spanning lodging renders as a passive staying-at row, never a hero
Pre-trip shows countdown + packing/documents/offline-pack nudges; empty day, day-complete (tomorrow preview) and trip-ended states each render on their condition
Quick actions open the map tab, expense modal, and photo capture; timeline item tap cross-tab pushes to its detail

features.title: Weather: cache-first endpoint + today strip with honest staleness
verification: GET /trips/:tripId/weather serves from weather_cache within TTL (no provider call — mock assert); expired/missing refetches through the provider port and upserts
Provider failure serves the stale blob with its fetched_at (client shows the age); no forecast hides the strip without blocking the timeline
Units render per UserPrefs.units (conversion is presentation)

features.title: Active-trip offline bundle: full today/tab render with staleness honesty
verification: Wifi + trip within 3 days of start: bundle (itinerary, bookings, saved places+rows, legs, ready tour bundles, member profiles, weather snapshot, trip row) downloads once automatically; cellular auto-download never fires; manual download works from trip settings with a size warning

Airplane mode: today tab renders completely from SQLite (timeline, hero, leave-by, weather snapshot, quick actions) with zero spinners; itinerary/map/money tabs mount from cache; deeplink-out buttons disable with an offline hint
Offline pill shows last-sync age; volatile surfaces (weather, transit) show their fetched-at age past TTL
Online itinerary change triggers an incremental bundle refresh

features.title: Offline mutation queue: FIFO drain, LWW, visible failed changes
verification: Offline: add an expense and check off packing items — both apply optimistically and enqueue (persisted OfflineMutation entries survive app kill)
Reconnect: queue drains FIFO per trip through the descriptor-addressed ApiClient; server truth reconciles the cache after drain
A server-rejected mutation surfaces in the failed-changes banner with retry and discard (discard rolls back the optimistic patch); nothing silently dropped
itinerary_change push while online invalidates mapped queries; the actor's own echo triggers no banner

features.title: Push registration, priming, preferences
verification: Notification priming renders before the OS permission dialog; denial degrades visibly (no silent null token) and the EAS projectId path is exercised on a real build
Token registers on grant and re-registers on foreground (last_seen_at bumps); sign-out deletes the token and cancels local schedules
Prefs screen round-trips GET/PUT notification prefs; a disabled category is never sent (server-side check per send)

features.title: Send pipeline hygiene: receipts, PII posture, no LLM, entitlement seam
verification: Every sent payload validates against the shared NotificationPayload union and routes through the deep-link registry shape (ids, never entity content)
DeviceNotRegistered receipts delete the token; stale tokens pruned by the job
Templates contain no amounts-with-counterparty-PII beyond the pinned posture; CI check proves no LLM import in notification/job modules; proactive-alert categories consult the entitlement seam

features.title: Collaborator itinerary-change pushes with coalescing
verification: Member A edits itinerary items: member B receives the push (actor excluded); burst edits within the 120s window coalesce into one notification
Notification tap lands on the item detail (single) or trip default tab (coalesced); foreground arrival invalidates queries silently

features.title: Scheduled sends: day-ahead digest, document expiry, settle-up request
verification: Active trip: digest fires at 20:00 destination-local (device-tz fallback) with tomorrow's first item + counts, deduped per (user, trip, day) — drive with a faked clock
Document with expiry + remind_days_before: reminder fires once (last_reminded_at dedup) and taps into the vault
Creating a settle-up request pushes the debtor and taps into the request screen

features.title: Leave-by local notifications from the offline bundle
verification: Bundle with legs: local notifications schedule at leave_by for items in the 48h window (<=64 pending cap respected); items without legs/start times skipped
Reschedule on foreground/bundle refresh/local mutation; pref off schedules nothing
Tapping a leave-by notification lands on today with the hero focused on that item; missing item falls back to plain today

features.title: Notification tap-routing through the deep-link registry with guards
verification: Each category routes per the §2.8 table on cold AND warm start identically (drive each category with a test push)
Signed-out tap stashes and resumes after auth; a stale notification for a trip the user left renders the no-access state
Missing-entity fallbacks land per table (default tab / money tab / documents list)

features.title: Documents vault: strictly private, presigned flows, expiry metadata
verification: Owner CRUDs documents with presigned upload and short-TTL signed download; metadata-only entries (no scan) work as reminder rows
A co-member of the associated trip cannot read the document by any route (ownership matrix: owner/member/stranger x endpoint — member and stranger 404)
Upload validation enforces type/size; reminder scheduling persists and dedups via last_reminded_at
No document scan is cached offline (v1 posture — storage inspection)

features.title: Packing lists: shared per trip, whole-list edits, offline check-off, AI seed
verification: Members read the shared list; editor+ adds/edits/checks items via whole-list PATCH keyed by stable item ids; viewer read-only per matrix
Concurrent PATCHes resolve LWW without corrupting the items array
Offline check-off applies optimistically and drains through the mutation queue
Generate-with-AI seeds the list (ai_generated=true) and user edits persist; regenerate offers replace, not silent overwrite

features.title: Payment-rail device tests D1-D4 on real hardware
verification: D1: Venmo txn=charge on real iPhone + Android — result recorded; request-via-Venmo stays disabled unless it passes
D2: Venmo return-to-app behavior after payment validates the return-prompt trigger
D3: Cash App/PayPal universal links open externally (never in-app browser) from our app context
D4: canOpenURL gating verified on one device WITH Venmo (scheme path) and one WITHOUT (web fallback)

features.title: Android verification pass
verification: On a real Android device: share-target ingestion, deep links (cold/warm), back-button stack-pop parity, map rendering + offline packs, push delivery, and the payment-rail queries element all exercised with results recorded
Any Android-specific failures filed as B-N bugs and fixed before store submission

features.title: Universal-link domain live: AASA/assetlinks verified
verification: Sean's purchased domain replaces links.gogotravel.example via the single LINK_DOMAIN config constant (one-config diff)
AASA and assetlinks serve correctly; invite and settle-request links open the installed app directly from Messages/Mail on device (cold + warm)
gogo:// scheme fallback still routes

features.title: EAS production build + TestFlight with zero embedded secrets
verification: EAS production iOS build succeeds and installs via TestFlight; the app runs the full golden path against the production API
Bundle inspection: no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, no server-side secrets, no Mapbox secret-scope token in the shipped artifact
Server deploys with env-only secrets (Law #1 audit of the deploy config)

features.title: App Store readiness: privacy labels, mandated flows, permission copy
verification: Privacy nutrition labels match reality: foreground-only location, photo/EXIF handling, 30-day capture raw retention disclosed
Account deletion is reachable and functional in the shipped build (guideline 5.1.1(v)); Apple token revocation fires
Every permission prompt carries its priming copy; app review metadata/screenshots prepared; privacy policy published with the capture retention window

features.title: Large-trip performance floor
verification: Fixture trip with 500 saved places, 14 days, 100+ itinerary items, 500 photos: map pans at interactive framerates (style-layer pins, clustering on), itinerary and today lists scroll without jank (virtualized), album grid paginates smoothly — measured on device, numbers recorded
Cold launch to interactive trip list under an agreed budget on a mid-tier device
